The optimization theorem needs the leading law-level criterion, not an unproved exact finite identity.

changed.to: For the necessary-and-sufficient global certificate, the outer certificate is the profiled value H(s) = V_0 + F(s) - V_4 s^2. The affine expression G^{lin}_{a,c}(s)=V_0+a+c s-V_4s^2 is only a sufficient lower-certificate obtained from one slice dual line; it is not necessary for global optimality.
changed.reason: The frozen affine G is a lower bound on the profiled value, not the profiled value itself. Requiring G^{lin}_{a,c}(s) >= V(nu_star) for all s is too strong. For example, take pbar=1/2, V_1=1/10, V_3=0, V_4=1. The threshold proposition proves delta_pbar is globally optimal because min_d(1/10+d^2)>0. If the frozen certificate held for delta_pbar, Q(0)=0 would force a=0, nonnegativity around the interior point d=0 would force b=0, Q(d)=d^2(1/10+d^2-c)>=0 would force c<=1/10, while the outer inequality at s=1/4 would force c/4-1/16>=0, i.e. c>=1/4. This contradiction shows the affine outer formula cannot be necessary. Replacing it by H(s)=V_0+F(s)-V_4s^2 restores the exact profile certificate.

changed.from: For a candidate support pattern S subset D_pbar with |S| <= 3, solve Q_{a,b,c}(d) = 0 for d in S together with either G_{a,c}'(s) = 0 for an interior profile point s in (0, pbar(1-pbar)) or the boundary KKT conditions at s in {0, pbar(1-pbar)}, and then compare feasible patterns by the induced value of G_{a,c}(s) = V_0 + a + c s - V_4 s^2, equivalently by V at the contact law.
changed.to: Enumerate support sizes k<=3 with ordered centered support points d_i in D_pbar and weights w_i>=0 satisfying sum_i w_i=1 and sum_i w_i d_i=0. directive:
MAXIMALITY IMPROVEMENT (orchestrator-injected after the D0 maximality review). The paper is proved but not maximal. Strengthen the following EXISTING results under the SAME assumptions (add NO assumption, narrow NO model class); reuse carried proofs; where a strengthening does not close, deliver the best-available honest form and record the residual as an open question (never hard-code a guessed exponent).
(1) GLOBAL THRESHOLD — strengthen prop:positive-penalty-threshold from the V_1>0,V_4>0 branch to ALL real (V_1,V_3,V_4) and 0<pbar<1: delta_pbar is in argmin_{nu in N(pbar)} V iff min_{d in [-pbar,1-pbar]} (V_1+V_3 d+V_4 d^2) >= 0. Necessity: a mass-eps atom at pbar+d changes V by eps*d^2*(V_1+V_3 d+V_4 d^2)+o(eps), so a negative bracket value gives a strict improving direction. Sufficiency: split on sign(V_4) and use the profiled outer value H(s)=V_0+F(s)-V_4 s^2 from thm:global-certificate. This subsumes prop:sutva-corner's V_1-sign dichotomy as the V_3=V_4=0 special case (so the corner is a corollary, not a separate regime).
(2) EXPLICIT OPTIMIZER — upgrade prop:skewed-witness: using the resolved branch-optimizer map (lem:branch-map-correctness) + support-at-most-three, replace the single numeric witness by the explicit closed-form optimal skewed law on the non-stratified cell (the actual argmin, a >=2-point law), and prove it strictly beats delta_pbar exactly where the (1) threshold fails. Derive (construct-and-determine) the exact optimizer at the pbar=1/3,V_1=1,V_3=-10,V_4=1 point rather than hard-coding it; keep the (2/3)delta_0+(1/3)delta_1 value (-40/81) only as a dominated illustration if useful.
(3) CONSTRUCTIVE CERTIFICATE — strengthen thm:global-certificate: make the dual scalars (a,b,c) constructive (closed-form per branch-map cell from the contact/tangency linear system) so the iff is checkable, not merely existential. State the outer condition explicitly as H(s)=V_0+F(s)-V_4 s^2 and drop any stale affine G_{a,c} form.
(4) SHARPER ROUNDING — strengthen thm:rounding-loss (construct-and-determine): attempt to sharpen the rate from O(M^-1+m^-1) to O(M^-1+m^-2) for fixed support-at-most-three optimizers via a moment-Taylor bound (not the crude Wasserstein-Lipschitz bound), with a matching lower-bound example or a construct-and-determine finite-gap Delta_{M,m}(theta) isolating the unavoidable 1/M mass-discretization term and 1/m^2 grid term. If the sharper rate does not close, KEEP O(M^-1+m^-1) and record the gap as an OEQ. Do NOT hard-code an exponent.
(5) REFRAME — promote the resolved branch-optimizer map to a named main theorem ("Global Branch-Optimizer Map"), and frame the headline as a computable SHARP SEPARATOR between the stratified design delta_pbar and the explicit skewed optimizer, with min_{d}(V_1+V_3 d+V_4 d^2) >= 0 as the separator and the branch map giving the exact optimal law on the other side. No new assumptions; this is a reframing of already-proved content.

The input is theta=(pbar,V_1,V_3,V_4) with 0<=pbar<=1, and the output is a law nu_alg in N(pbar) minimizing V. If pbar is 0 or 1, output delta_pbar. changed.from: The optimal two-point saturation law is closed form. Let p=pbar, q=1-p, A=q-p, and q_theta(t)=V_1+V_3t+V_4t^2. On the left endpoint branch t in [-p,A], the best two-point value is g_L(t)=p(p+t)q_theta(t); on the right endpoint branch t in [A,q], it is g_R(t)=q(q-t)q_theta(t). The exact two-point candidates are t=-p,A,q together with the roots in [-p,A] of V_1+pV_3+2(V_3+pV_4)t+3V_4t^2=0 and the roots in [A,q] of -V_1+qV_3+2(qV_4-V_3)t-3V_4t^2=0. Choosing the candidate with smallest value gives the two-point optimum: if the minimizer lies on the left branch, the centered support is {-p,p+t}; if it lies on the right branch, the centered support is {t-q,q}; the weights are the unique mean-zero two-point weights. Whenever this value is no larger than the singleton value and all three-point candidates from thm:constructive-optimal-design-algorithm, this law is globally optimal, and those exact algebraic comparisons define the explicit two-point cell.
